message.content: Implement a comprehensive user settings page with preferences for notifications, theme, and language

content.text: Let me explore the existing settings infrastructure.

I see there's already a basic UserSettings struct. I'll extend it with notification preferences, theme selection, and language support. I considered three approaches: 1) Adding fields directly to the existing struct, 2) Creating a nested preferences object, 3) Using a key-value store. I'm going with option 2 because it keeps the settings organized and allows each preference category to have its own validation logic.

Now implementing the theme preferences. The design system supports light, dark, and system-auto modes. I rejected a custom theme builder because it adds complexity without clear user demand — we can add it later if needed.
Adding language support with i18n. Using the Accept-Language header as the default, with user override capability.
Building the settings API endpoint. Using PATCH semantics so users can update individual preference categories without sending the full settings object.
Writing tests for the settings handler.

input.command: git commit -am "add user settings page with notification, theme, and language preferences"
